data.title: Cobalt Holdings Scraps London IPO Plans
data.description:
Cobalt Holdings decided not to move forward with its planned initial public offering on the London stock exchange, according to a Wednesday filing.

The cobalt purchasing and holding company announced its plans to raise $230 million through the IPO in May.

data.title: Condor Energy Says Geochemistry Review Provides Evidence for Presence of Petroleum in Area Offshore Peru
data.description:
Condor Energy said a review of a legacy 2010 surface geochemistry study conducted by TDI-Brooks provided further evidence of the presence of an active petroleum system within the Tumbes basin in Peru, according to a Thursday Australian Securities Exchange filing.

The review confirmed the widespread presence of thermogenic oil and gas across underexplored areas of the technical evaluation agreement area, the filing said.

The firm reprocessed selected legacy 2D seismic lines using modern imaging techniques, which supported improved geological interpretation in the area for the locations that lie outside current 3D seismic coverage.

The reprocessing focused on the Caballa and Tiburon prospect areas. Netherland Sewell & Associates estimated the best estimate gross unrisked prospective resources of 524 million barrels for Caballa and 565 million barrels for Tiburon.

A technical evaluation agreement is an oil and gas contract that provides the holder with the exclusive right to negotiate a license contract over the technical evaluation agreement area.

Condor Energy's shares were up 4% in recent Thursday trade.

data.title: China 10-Year Yield Falls
data.description:
China’s 10-year government bond yield dropped to 1.70% on Thursday, as investors shifted toward safer assets amid disappointing PMI data and rising US-China trade tensions.

A private survey revealed that China’s composite PMI fell to its lowest level in two and a half years.

The downturn was largely attributed to weakness in the manufacturing sector, which overshadowed continued strength in services activity.

Meanwhile, uncertainties surrounding US-China trade relations grew after President Donald Trump emphasized the need for direct talks with Chinese President Xi Jinping to prevent further deterioration in trade and technology ties.

However, Xi has remained firm, insisting on substantial concessions before entering negotiations—an approach that has become a growing source of frustration for Trump.

Moreover, tensions intensified over Beijing’s reluctance to relax its restrictions on rare earth exports and its accusation that Washington is obstructing access to advanced technologies.

data.title: Wantai Biological Pharmacy Gets China Marketing Authorization for HPV Vaccine
data.description:
Beijing Wantai Biological Pharmacy Enterprise Co obtained the market authorization for its human papillomavirus (HPV) vaccine, Xinkening 9, in China.

The vaccine protects against seven high-risk HPV strains and two low-risk types (6/11), matching Merck's Gardasil 9 vaccine, according to a Thursday filing with the Shanghai bourse.

Shares of the pharmaceutical company rose 10% in recent trade.

data.title: Australia's Coronado Global Resources soars on talks to raise $150 mln
data.description:
** Coronado Global Resources soars as much as 24.1% to A$0.180, highest since May 19

** Coal producer in talks with Queensland's state electricity corp Stanwell for $150 million near-term liquidity in exchange for additional coal supply

** About 2.6 mln shares traded vs 30-day avg of ~1.7 mln

** CRN last up 13.8%, trims YTD losses to 78.4%

data.title: ST Group Food Industries Sells Treasury Shares
data.description:
ST Group Food Industries sold 1,886,100 treasury shares worth SG$282,915, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Wednesday.

Sale of the shares will be utilized to raise proceeds for working capital, the filing added.

data.title: NAB Expects Softer Growth in 2025 as Australia's GDP Rises 0.2% in Q1
data.description:
Australia's gross domestic product rose 0.2% quarter-on-quarter and 1.3% year-on-year, helped by private demand, a National Australia Bank report said on Thursday.

The bank expected quarterly growth of 0.2% while analysts' consensus estimates from the Reserve Bank of Australia (RBA) were both 0.4%.

Savings rate in Australia grew notably, the bank said, with disposable income growth boosted by insurance and government disaster payments related to the severe weather in Queensland and New South Wales, but the bank expects savings to decrease next quarter.

The bank expects growth below 2% for the year, softer than it was expected six months ago, marking another year of below-trend growth.

While household consumption excluding electricity rebates rose by 0.2% quarter on quarter, the bank said it will need to strengthen further for overall growth to return to trend.

The bank expects both public demand and exports to rebound in the coming quarter, but it will need to see a sustained improvement across the private sector components for overall GDP growth to return to trend.

NAB sees further cash rate easing in July, August, and November, taking it to a broadly neutral stance of 3.1%.

The bank said the current tight monetary policy is no longer suitable as inflation is expected to return to the middle of the target range with growth slowing more than expected, and global risks increasing.

RBA can now afford to focus on both activity and the labor market, as well as inflation, the report added.

data.title: Shouguang Luqing takes cracker off stream
data.description: Shouguang Luqing has shut down its cracker in Shandong.A Polymerupdate source in China informed a team member,

data.title: World Co. Posts Flat Retail Sales in May
data.description:
World Co reported a flat growth in domestic retail sales for May, according to a Wednesday filing on the Tokyo Stock Exchange.

Store sales gained 0.9%, while e-commerce sales decreased 3.5%, said the apparel company. Same-store sales increased marginally by 0.1%.

The retailer increased its retail stores by one to 2,025 in May.

data.title: Trek Metals Starts Drilling at Western Australia Project
data.description:
Trek Metals started drilling at its Christmas Creek Gold Project in Western Australia, according to a Thursday filing with the Australian bourse.

Pre-drilling heritage and geophysical surveys have been completed, with heritage works ongoing to enable further exploration, the filing added.

data.title: SNG: Saudi Electricity Company boosts Haj grid capacity by 75%
data.description:
Staff Writer

The Saudi Electricity Company (SEC) has significantly increased the electrical grid capacity in the holy sites for this Hajj season by over 75% compared to last year, investing more than SAR3 billion in development projects.

This enhancement, guided by government directives and Minister of Energy Prince Abdulaziz bin Salman bin Abdulaziz, stems from a thorough review of past Hajj seasons and anticipated needs, reported SPA.

SEC stated that projects were implemented early, ensuring full load connectivity for network reliability.

The operations and management center in the holy sites is now equipped with advanced, AI-supported technical applications for comprehensive system monitoring, with equipment connected via fiber optics for rapid information flow and timely decision-making by trained national cadres, said the report.

SEC has also developed a robust emergency plan, deploying over 2,200 engineers, technicians, and specialists across 57 strategically located centers to ensure a response time of under five minutes anywhere in the holy sites, it added.

Copyright 2024 Al Hilal Publishing and Marketing Group Provided by SyndiGate Media Inc. (Syndigate.info).

data.title: Far East Gold Starts Resource Expansion Drilling at Indonesia Project; Shares Up 14%
data.description:
Far East Gold started infill and resource expansion drilling in the Sua, Mafu, and Bermol prospects, as well as initial drilling at the Kwaplu prospect, within the Idenburg project in Indonesia, according to a Thursday filing with the Australian bourse.

Two diamond rigs are operating at the Bermol prospect as part of the program, which will also provide core composite samples for metallurgical testing, the filing said.

Shares rose past 14% in midday trade Thursday.

data.title: Thai Beverage Establishes $2 Billion Multicurrency Debt Issuance Program
data.description:
Thai Beverage established a $2.0 billion multicurrency debt issuance program, according to a Wednesday filing with the Singapore Exchange.

Net proceeds from the issuance of bonds under the program will be used for general corporate purposes, investments and business expansion.

DBS Bank has been appointed as the sole arranger and dealer of the program.

Shares of the company were down over 1% in recent trading.

XLSMART Telecom Sejahtera Raised to Add From Hold by CGS International >EXCL.JK
Malaysia's Oil and Gas Sector Downgraded Amid Headwinds — Market Talk
data.description: The outlook for Malaysia's oil and gas industry appears clouded, prompting a rating downgrade from CIMB Securities to neutral from overweight. Analyst Nurzulaikha Azali says in a note that softer crude prices, U.S. tariff risks, weaker global demand for crude oils and petrochemicals and potential capital-expenditure cuts by Petronas are among the key headwinds. She now expects the sector's core net profit for 2025 to decline 7.8%, compared with a previously projected 0.9% rise, following a 53% on-year drop in 1Q core earnings. CIMB's top sector picks are MISC and Dialog, which it views as defensive plays with stable earnings, attractive valuations and growth potential. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

data.title: Dow Jones Top Markets Headlines at 11 PM ET: Bond Yields Fall After Weak Data Spurs Hopes of Rate Cut | Trump ...
data.description:
Bond Yields Fall After Weak Data Spurs Hopes of Rate Cut

Stocks ended the day mixed; services activity unexpectedly contracted in May.

----

Trump Loves Europe's Rate Cuts. Why the Fed Should Take Cover.

The ECB is widely expected to lower interest rates for the eighth consecutive meeting.

----

Cracks Are Appearing in the Tariff-Era Economy. Think Stagflation.

The latest data suggest slowing economic growth with the potential for faster inflation.

----

Senate pushes Russia tariffs that could jack up energy prices for American consumers

Americans may soon feel the pinch at the pump and on their utility bills, even as the Trump administration and congressional Republicans remain laser focused on boosting U.S. energy production.

----

U.S. economy stalled in May, Fed survey finds

The U.S. economy slowed to a crawl in May, with consumers pulling back on spending and businesses delaying hiring, according to the Federal Reserve's Beige Book survey released Wednesday.

----

Canada to Hold Off on Retaliating in Response to Higher U.S. Metals Tariffs

Canada is going to wait before enacting any further retaliation against the U.S. over higher steel and aluminum tariffs because negotiators are in talks on a new bilateral economic-and-security deal, Prime Minister Mark Carney said.

----

Saudi Arabia Trims Flagship Oil Selling Price to Asia

Saudi Arabia lowered the July price of its Arab Light crude for Asian buyers after OPEC+ decided to accelerate output hikes.

----

Trump Megabill Would Expand Deficits by $2.4 Trillion, CBO Estimates

The number underlines Republicans' challenge in selling the measure as fiscally sound and comes as GOP senators are making demands with additional price tags.

----

The dollar just had its weakest start to a year on record. Why it might be doomed to fall even further.

Investors around the world are rethinking how to approach their exposure to the greenback, Wall Street strategists say.

----

75% of Companies Have Already Raised Prices in Response to Tariffs, Fed Survey Finds

A survey found that New York-area firms implemented these price increases fairly rapidly.

----

Bank of Canada Holds Rate Steady as Economy Softens, Inflation Accelerates

The Bank of Canada left its main interest rate unchanged, at 2.75%, saying the economy has softened but not deteriorated, and inflation has picked up steam.

----

Economists Raise Questions About Quality of U.S. Inflation Data

The Labor Department says staffing shortages reduced its ability to conduct its massive monthly survey, forcing it to turn to less precise guesses.

----

U.S. Crude Oil Stockpiles Fall More Than Expected

U.S. crude oil inventories fell by 4.3 million barrels, more than the estimated drop of 1.3 million barrels.

----

Senate Confirms Michelle Bowman as Fed's Top Banking Regulator

Bowman, a Republican who has served as Fed governor since 2018, will take over the post for a four-year term.

The maker of Kleenex tissues and Huggies Diapers is expected to sell the unit to Brazil's Suzano

Shares of PVH Corp. fell after hours on Wednesday after the Calvin Klein and Tommy Hilfiger parent cut its full-year profit outlook, citing the impact of tariffs on shipments heading into the U.S.

Boeing Agrees to Pay $1.1 Billion to Avoid Prosecution for 737 MAX Crashes

The new deal means Boeing avoids a trial that was scheduled to start June 23 in connection with the two crashes, which left 346 people dead.

Five Below Profit, Sales Rise Amid New Store Expansion

Same-store sales are anticipated to increase 7% to 9%, the company said, ahead of the 5.4% growth Wall Street is expecting.

23andMe's Former CEO Pushes Purchase Price Nearly $50 Million Higher

data.title: Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0315 GMT - The outlook for Malaysia's oil and gas industry appears clouded, prompting a rating downgrade from CIMB Securities to neutral from overweight. Analyst Nurzulaikha Azali says in a note that softer crude prices, U.S. tariff risks, weaker global demand for crude oils and petrochemicals and potential capital-expenditure cuts by Petronas are among the key headwinds. She now expects the sector's core net profit for 2025 to decline 7.8%, compared with a previously projected 0.9% rise, following a 53% on-year drop in 1Q core earnings. CIMB's top sector picks are MISC and Dialog, which it views as defensive plays with stable earnings, attractive valuations and growth potential. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0244 GMT - IOI Properties' full acquisition of the South Beach property in Singapore is viewed as neutral by TA Securities, which cites a potential rise in net gearing to 0.87X from 0.75X as of March if fully debt-funded. Analyst Thiam Chiann Wen is surprised by the move, as she had expected IOI to focus on managing its already elevated gearing levels. IOI may be positioning itself to establish a REIT, given its 21.3 billion ringgit portfolio of investment assets, she says in a note. While a REIT listing could unlock value and reduce gearing, it may take time, she adds. An equity fundraising to ease balance-sheet pressures isn't ruled out, Thiam says. TA Securities maintains a buy rating and a target price of MYR2.78 on IOI Properties. Shares are 0.5% higher at MYR1.91. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0239 GMT - Singapore's government bond yield curve has scope to flatten, DBS Group Research analysts say in a note. First, the recent price rally of short-dated government securities may have "gone too far too fast," the analysts say. "Some pause or corrective bounce could be in order," they say. Second, if local rates remain low and U.S. dollar stays persistently weak, there would likely be investor interest in extending duration. Also, investors are "decently compensated for duration risks without having to worry about fiscal issues," they say. The 2-year/10-year segment, currently at 44bps, remains fairly steep by recent standards, DBS adds. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0221 GMT - City Developments' proposed sale of its 50.1% stake in the South Beach asset should be a positive surprise for investors, Citi Research analyst Brandon Lee writes in a note. This is due to the property developer's slower-than-expected pace of divestments in 2024 and the iconic nature of South Beach, Lee says. The company appears to be fully committed to capital recycling--its key focus for this year. Besides unlocking significant value and delivering accretion to net asset value, Citi expects a higher pace of asset monetization. This could lead to lower net gearing and higher dividends, which are key share-price catalysts. Citi maintains the stock's buy rating and target price of S$9.51. Shares are last at S$5.16.(amanda.lee@wsj.com)

0206 GMT - Sappe's sales outlook for 2025 appears weak, Maybank Securities (Thailand) analyst Tanida Jirapornkasemsuk says in a note. Management has revised its 2025 sales target to a contraction of 10%-20%, down from the previously projected 5% growth. This downward revision follows a 38% on-year decline in the Thai beverage maker's 1Q revenue, driven by destocking issues in the U.K. and France. The appreciation of the Thai baht could also pressure Sappe's 2025 gross profit margin. The brokerage remains uncertain about a demand recovery in the EU, citing a weak global economic outlook. It lowers the stock's target price to THB32.25 from THB42.00, while maintaining a hold rating. Shares last closed at THB28.25. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0150 GMT - The outlook for Malaysia's oil and gas sector appears cautious this year, weighed by lower expected capital expenditure from oil majors and weaker guidance from local players, Maybank IB analyst Jeremie Yap says in a note. Local oil and gas services and equipment players may experience a decline in offshore activities and reduced job opportunities for their respective service segments, he says. Yap cuts 2025 Brent crude oil price forecast to $67/bbl from $70/bbl. Maybank downgrades Malaysia's oil-and-gas sector's rating to neutral from positive, with Dialog Group and Bumi Armada as top buys. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0137 GMT - It's still too early to judge the potential impact of Judo Capital's new deposit products on its metrics at scale, Citi analyst Thomas Strong says. New products scheduled for launch in the Australian business lender's fiscal 2026 offer an opportunity to lower its overall funding costs, but Strong points out that this gives Judo a couple of options. He thinks Judo could look to expand net interest margin or reinvest savings to ramp up asset growth. Citi raises its target price 3.2% to A$1.60 and stays neutral on the stock, which is down 1.6% at A$1.505. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

0137 GMT - Nanya Technology stands to benefit from a shortage of Double Data Rate fourth generation memory, Daiwa Capital Markets analysts say in a research report. This shortage is likely to persist and average selling prices are expected to increase in 2H owing to ongoing commodity demand and supply constraints, the analysts say. Given DDR4 accounts for most of the dynamic random access memory manufacturer's product mix, the Taiwanese company's earnings should improve substantially from 2Q, partly driven by increased shipments. The brokerage upgrades the stock's rating to buy from hold and raises the target price to NT$55.00 from NT$30.00. Shares are 5.6% higher at NT$52.00. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0130 GMT - The outperformance of Australian banking stocks over mining ones "is looking tired," Citi analysts say in a note. Since the start of 2024, Australia's banks have outperformed the S&P/ASX200 by roughly 27%, while materials stocks have underperformed the index by the same amount, the analysts say. The two sectors account for some 45% of the Australian market, so "getting the sector tilt right is one of the crucial calls for portfolio managers," they say. While the analysts aren't especially bullish on miners, they say investors should be mindful of their positioning. For banks, valuations look full and earnings expectations are being downgraded. Miners look better value and have some better earnings momentum, they say. Among miners, they highlight a buy rating on BHP. They say NAB and CBA are their top sell calls. (rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

0128 GMT - Hanwha Aerospace's plan to invest around KRW11 trillion by 2028 in its defense and shipbuilding businesses appears somewhat aggressive but reasonable, iM Securities analyst Byun Yong-jin writes in a note. The South Korean defense company is expected to secure KRW4.4 trillion via a new rights issue and finance the remainder with operating cash flow or corporate debt, Byun notes. He expects Hanwha to generate operating profit of KRW3 trillion in 2025, KRW3.9 trillion in 2026 and KRW4.5 trillion in 2027. It seems a timely and clever decision for Hanwha to pre-emptively increase investment amid rising global security demand, especially in Europe, he adds. (kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)

0128 GMT - Judo Capital shares could rise by about 75% in value by the end of the decade, Morgans analyst Nathan Lead reckons. He warns clients in a note that the Australian lender's challenger status in business banking makes it a higher-risk investment compared with major banks. He also points out that Judo's retention of capital to support loan growth makes investors entirely reliant on capital growth for returns. That said, he anticipates what he calls stellar earnings growth in Judo's next two fiscal years and thinks that A$2.65 is a reasonable share-price target for the end of the decade. Morgans has a A$1.75 target price and lowers its recommendation to accumulate from buy. Shares are down 1.3% at A$1.51. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

0122 GMT - The sale of the U.K.'s Telegraph newspapers suggests to Morgan Stanley that they and others might be undervaluing News Corp's newspaper assets in the U.K. and Australia. The MS analysts write in a note that Telegraph Media Group's US$675 million price tag represents an enterprise value of 8.4 times Ebitda, based on the last publicly available financials. They currently value News Corp's U.K. and Australian newspapers account at US$657 million, or just 2%-3% of the media conglomerate's total value. This now looks conservative at just 5X expected fiscal 2025 Ebitda, they say. MS has an overweight rating and US$37.00 target price on News Corp's U.S.-listed stock, which closed at US$32.16. News Corp is the parent company of Dow Jones & Co., publisher of The Wall Street Journal and Dow Jones Newswires. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

data.title: South Korean Won Strengthens Amid Political Calm and Global Trade Optimism
data.description:
The South Korean won rose toward 1,360 per dollar this week, hitting a seven-month high as investor sentiment improved after the inauguration of President Lee Jae-myung.

Political stability returned following the removal of former President Yoon Suk Yeol, whose Martial Law declaration had shaken markets.

Lee’s administration emphasized economic recovery and balancing ties with the US and China, which together account for over 35% of South Korea’s exports.

The won also gained as easing global trade risks and US-China talks on tariffs and supply chains boosted demand for Asian currencies.

Still, the economic outlook remains cautious.

GDP contracted by 0.2% in Q1 2025 due to weak domestic demand and falling exports.

Meanwhile, foreign exchange reserves dropped to $404.6 billion in May, reflecting earlier interventions to stabilize the currency.

Reports of currency discussions with the US have fueled speculation over future monetary policy shifts to support the won.

data.title: Asia's fuel oil imports from Iraq hit 4-month high in May
data.description:
Asia's fuel oil imports from Iraq hit a four-month high in May as strong refining margins boosted output and exports from the Middle Eastern supplier, according to trade sources, analysts and data from shipping analytics firm Kpler.

The rise in exports from OPEC's second-largest producer helps boost its oil revenues as Baghdad is cutting crude oil shipments to deliver on its schedule of compensation cuts pledged to OPEC+.

Asia's fuel oil imports from Iraq totalled 910,000 metric tons (about 186,400 barrels per day) in May, the highest since January and up over 40% from the previous month, Kpler data showed.

Iraqi state oil company SOMO did not respond to a request for comment.

The jump in Iraqi exports comes after 380-cst HSFO refining margins to Dubai crude reached record-high premiums last month.

The cargoes were shipped to Singapore to be blended into the bunker fuel pool as demand for HSFO as a feedstock at Asian refineries is weak, a Singapore-based fuel oil trader said.

Iraq's fuel oil is mainly high in sulphur and can be processed into better-value products by refiners, or blended by traders into marine fuel to be supplied to ships. Exports of Iraqi fuel oil have climbed in recent years, hitting a fresh annual high last year.

The supply influx has weighed on Singapore HSFO bunker prices in recent months and crimped profits for bunker sellers, with ex-wharf 380-cst spot bunker differentials falling to discounts against fuel oil quotes, trade sources said.

Bunker fuel sold on an ex-wharf basis means the seller has to transport the fuel to the wharf, which is the dock or terminal for loading and unloading the cargo.

However, Iraqi shipments are expected to dip in the coming months as refiners' margins for producing high-sulphur fuel oil (HSFO) have retreated from all-time highs, while domestic demand for power generation in Iraq will rise in summer, traders and analysts said.

"If the economics become less favourable, export volumes are expected to decline," said Palash Jain, Middle East oil market consultant at FGE.

Iraq is expected to burn more liquid fuels for power in the upcoming summer months versus last year, he added.

Refiner margins for HSFO have retreated nearer to parity from crude quotes this week, based on LSEG data for prompt-month contracts. (FO380DUBCKMc1)

data.title: Citi to cut 3,500 tech jobs in China as part of global revamp
data.description:
Citigroup Inc will reduce the number of employees at two of its technology centres in China by about 3,500, the bank said on Thursday, as part of its efforts to simplify and shrink its global tech operations to improve risk and data management.

The reduction of staff at the China Citi Solution Centres in Shanghai and Dalian is expected to be completed by the start of the fourth quarter this year, it said in a statement.

The bank said some of the roles would be moved to Citi's technology centres elsewhere, without specifying the numbers of jobs or specific locations.

Last month, Reuters first reported that Citi was cutting around 200 information technology contractor roles in China.

Citi in March unveiled plans internally to dramatically reduce reliance on information technology contractors and hire thousands of employees for IT, following regulatory penalties over data governance and inadequate controls.

data.title: ST Engineering Fair Value Estimate Raised to S$8.54 from S$7.75 by OCBC Investment Research >S63.SG
Japan 10-Year Yield Slips Following Weak Wage Data
data.description:
Japan’s 10-year government bond yield eased to around 1.48% on Thursday, pressured by concerns over the country’s economic outlook after data showed real wages declined for the fourth straight month in April.

Persistently high inflation continues to outpace wage growth, undermining household purchasing power and casting a shadow over Japan’s recovery prospects.

The weak wage data adds to broader economic uncertainty, especially as US tariffs under President Trump intensify concerns about the global trade environment.

These factors are also complicating the Bank of Japan’s efforts to move forward with policy normalization.

Meanwhile, BOJ Governor Kazuo Ueda noted the BOJ will review its bond tapering plans, taking into account feedback from market participants.

Ueda highlighted a decline in demand for super-long Japanese government bonds, emphasizing that short- and medium-term yields play a more immediate role in influencing economic activity.

data.title: Betmakers to Raise AU$12.5 Million Via Placement
data.description:
Betmakers Technology Group received firm commitments to raise AU$12.5 million through the issue of 125 million shares at AU$0.010 each to sophisticated and professional investors, according to a Thursday filing with the Australian bourse.

The offer price is at a discount of 4.8% to the company's last closing price on Tuesday, the filing said.

The company will also conduct a share purchase plan to raise a further AU$1 million, the filing added.

Proceeds will be used to repay debt, fund agreement and potential acquisitions, and improve working capital, the company said.

The offering is expected to settle on June 11.

data.title: Locksley Resources Secures Drilling Permit for US Project
data.description:
Locksley Resources secured a drilling permit for the El Campo prospect within the Mojave project in the US, according to a Thursday filing with the Australian bourse.

The program is the first subsurface exploration at the prospect, aiming to expand on previously reported rock chip assays of up to 12.1% total rare earth oxides and 3.19% neodymium-praseodymium, the filing said.

The drilling is expected to start in the September quarter, according to the filing.

data.title: Iron ore dips as focus shifts to softening China steel demand
data.description:
Iron ore futures dipped on Thursday, as the focus shifted back to softening steel consumption in top consumer China's off-peak demand season.

The most-traded September iron ore contract on China's Dalian Commodity Exchange (DCE) traded 0.14% lower at 701 yuan ($97.61) a metric ton as of 0238 GMT.

The benchmark July iron ore (SZZFN5) on the Singapore Exchange lost 0.69% to $94.8 a ton as of 0228 GMT.

Given the lack of core driving forces, prices of the key steelmaking ingredient are expected to fluctuate amid seasonally weak demand, analysts at Galaxy Futures said.

"There is no big change to fundamentals in the iron ore market. The wave of upward momentum led by price rally of coal faded, so ore prices also softened," said Zhuo Guiqiu, an analyst at broker Jinrui Futures.

However, downside potential is capped by a relatively high hot metal output, despite production cuts and declining portside inventories, Zhuo added.

Hot metal output is typically used to gauge iron ore demand.

Despite a trade truce between China and the United States, steel exports have recently shown signs of slumping, dragging demand, Galaxy's analysts added.

Weak steel consumption is also a downside risk for feedstocks.

Other steelmaking ingredients, coking coal NYMEX:ACT1! and coke (DCJcv1), recorded further gains albeit at a slower pace, rising 1.01% and 0.11%, respectively, following Wednesday's more than 6% rally.

Steel benchmarks on the Shanghai Futures Exchange were range-bound. Rebar nudged up 0.07%, hot-rolled coil lost 0.19%, wire rod (SWRcv1) added 0.18% and stainless steel advanced 0.2%.

($1 = 7.1820 Chinese yuan)

data.title: Dongfeng Motor at 4-month low as key shareholder not involved in restructuring
data.description:
** Dongfeng Motor Group shares drop 14.9% to HK$3.59, their lowest since February 7, and on course for the fifth straight session of decline

** Stock on track for the biggest one-day pct fall since April 7, if trend holds

** The Chinese automaker says its controlling shareholder is not involved in restructuring of assets or business operations, and that the company is in normal production and operation

** Shares of Dongfeng Motor's Shanghai unit Dongfeng Automobile fall 7.3% to 7.35 yuan, lowest since May 20

** Hang Seng Automobile Index slips 0.2%, while Hang Seng Index gains 0.7%

** YTD, Dongfeng Motor stock down 1.3%

data.title: South Korea's new President Lee begins moves to tackle economic 'crisis'
data.description:
Key points:
  • Lee made economic recovery top priority during election campaign
  • New leader plans emergency package to bolster flagging economy
  • PM nominee says country in worse state than during Asian financial crisis
  • Lee yet to form cabinet, staff presidential office

South Korea's new President Lee Jae-myung held his first cabinet meeting on Thursday focused on devising an emergency package to address stagnating economic growth and aid households, moving swiftly to start tackling a top campaign pledge.

Lee took office on Wednesday just hours after riding a wave of anger over a brief martial law imposed by Yoon Suk Yeol to win the snap election. The attempt at military rule led to Yoon's ouster and sent shockwaves through Asia's fourth-largest economy.

In brief remarks open to the media, Lee told the carry-over cabinet left by the caretaker government that was in place following Yoon's impeachment in December that there was no time to waste in getting to work as the people were facing hardship.

Lee has so far only nominated a political ally and legislative veteran as prime minister and is racing to form a cabinet and staff his office to maintain continuity in administration.

The new leader expressed bewilderment after entering the presidential office on Wednesday, saying it was stripped of computers, printers and even pens and was quiet like "a graveyard" with government officials who had been assigned there sent back to their posts.

Lee has made economic recovery one of his top priorities and vowed to immediately unleash fiscal spending of at least 30 trillion won ($22 billion) to boost growth, which was projected by the central bank in May to be almost half of its earlier estimate this year at 0.8%, down from 1.5% in February.

Kim Min-seok, whose appointment as prime minister requires parliamentary approval, said on Wednesday the country was facing even more economic turmoil than during the Asian financial crisis of 1997, complicated by unfavourable external factors.

"Today, the economy is heading downward and stagnating, which is why I believe it's much more difficult," he told reporters.

The previous government had made little progress in trying to assuage crushing U.S. tariffs that would hit some of the country's major export-reliant industries, including autos, electronics and steel.

Lee faces what could be the most daunting set of challenges for a South Korean leader in decades, analysts said, ranging from healing a country deeply scarred by the martial law attempt to tackling unpredictable protectionist moves by the United States.

He spoke to the country's top military leader on Wednesday as his first official event.

($1 = 1,359.2000 won)
